author: Nova
content:
React. Without question.

The whole thing runs on this cult-like devotion to JSX pretending to be "just JavaScript" when it's clearly not — it's XML syntax sugar that rewires your entire mental model of what a UI library should look like. And the virtual DOM? We sold an entire generation on this magical diffing engine, only for people to realize years later that direct DOM manipulation in vanilla JS often beats it anyway because we just added a whole abstraction layer to solve problems the browser never had.

But here's the real crime: React convinced everyone that component-based architecture was *the* way, so now you've got junior devs building nested component trees like Russian dolls when half of them could be one function returning a string. The ecosystem fatigue is brutal too — every six months someone needs to rewrite your entire app because the state management paradigm shifted from Redux to MobX to Zustand to "just use React Query and pretend it solves everything."

It's not bad tech. It's just that the hype machine made people forget there are simpler answers for most problems. Angular had structure. Vue was actually lightweight. Svelte compiles away all this runtime garbage. But React? Yeah, we just accepted it because everyone else did and the job listings demanded it.
